arguments.query: Adding a New API Route add new API endpoint create route apps/web/src/app/api route.ts Zod tests pnpm lint type-check test route handler pattern requireAuth Zod service lib/db/queries import hierarchy

tool_results.content:
{'sections': [{'title': 'Adding a New API Route', 'content': '### Adding a New API Route

1. Create `apps/web/src/app/api/[resource]/route.ts`
2. Add to `lib/db/queries/` for database access
3. Add validation schema using Zod
4. Add tests in `route.test.ts`
5. Run: `pnpm lint && pnpm type-check && pnpm test`
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [344, 350], 'relevance_score': 1.0, 'token_count': 74, 'truncated': False}, {'title': '✅ Test 3: Existing API Keys', 'content': '### ✅ Test 3: Existing API Keys

**Status:** PASSED ✅

**What Was Tested:**

- Tested production API with valid project API key
- Endpoint: `POST https://api.snipara.com/v1/snipara/mcp`

**Results:**

- API responds (returns 500 for different reason - see below)
- Authentication layer accepts valid key
- No regressions in key validation logic

---
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [1454, 1469], 'relevance_score': 1.0, 'token_count': 90, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'API Routes (9 files)', 'content': '### API Routes (9 files)
```
apps/web/src/app/api/agents/
├── route.ts                       # GET overview stats
├── subscription/
│   ├── route.ts                   # GET/POST subscription
│   └── checkout/route.ts          # POST Stripe checkout
├── memory/
│   ├── route.ts                   # GET list, POST create
│   └── [id]/route.ts              # GET, DELETE
├── swarms/
│   ├── route.ts                   # GET list, POST create
│   └── [id]/
│       ├── route.ts               # GET, PATCH, DELETE
│       └── tasks/route.ts         # GET tasks
└── cache/
    └── route.ts                   # GET stats, DELETE invalidate
```
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [46954, 46971], 'relevance_score': 0.9181276768044205, 'token_count': 170, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'API Routes Created', 'content': '### API Routes Created
- `GET/POST/DELETE /api/projects/[projectId]/api-keys` - API key management
- `GET/PUT/DELETE /api/teams/[teamId]` - Team settings
- `GET/POST /api/invites/[token]` - Invite acceptance


### Schema Changes
- Added `notificationPrefs` JSON field to User model
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [15384, 15390], 'relevance_score': 0.8947462536917706, 'token_count': 80, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'Discovery API Routes', 'content': '### Discovery API Routes

| Route                                               | Method | Description                     |
| --------------------------------------------------- | ------ | ------------------------------- |
| `/api/shared-collections/[id]/discover`             | POST   | Trigger a discovery run         |
| `/api/shared-collections/[id]/discover`             | GET    | Get discovery runs and patterns |
| `/api/shared-collections/[id]/patterns/[patternId]` | PATCH  | Accept/reject a pattern         |
| `/api/shared-collections/[id]/patterns/[patternId]` | DELETE | Delete a pattern                |
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [32474, 32481], 'relevance_score': 0.8835951134380453, 'token_count': 116, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'Admin API Routes', 'content': '## Admin API Routes

Admin routes require platform admin authentication (`requirePlatformAdmin()`).

| Route                                              | Methods    | Description                              |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `/api/admin/users`                                 | GET        | List users with pagination and filtering |
| `/api/admin/users/[userId]`                        | GET, PATCH | Get or update user details               |
| `/api/admin/users/[userId]/ban`                    | POST       | Ban/unban a user                         |
| `/api/admin/stats`                                 | GET        | Platform-wide statistics                 |
| `/api/admin/audit`                                 | GET        | View admin audit logs                    |
| `/api/admin/subscriptions/[subscriptionId]`        | PATCH      | Update Context subscription              |
| `/api/admin/agents-subscriptions/[subscriptionId]` | PATCH      | Update Agents subscription               |

---
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [10850, 10864], 'relevance_score': 0.8727818865253419, 'token_count': 176, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'Automation API Routes', 'content': '## Automation API Routes

| Route                                                       | Methods | Description                    |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | ------------------------------ |
| `/api/projects/:projectIdOrSlug/automation`                 | GET/PUT | Get/update automation settings |
| `/api/projects/:projectIdOrSlug/automation/inject`          | POST    | Inject context (from hooks)    |
| `/api/projects/:projectIdOrSlug/automation/inject`          | GET     | Retrieve stored context        |
| `/api/projects/:projectIdOrSlug/automation/memory-settings` | GET     | Get memory injection settings  |
| `/api/mcp/:projectIdOrSlug`                                 | POST    | MCP tool proxy                 |

**Note:** All automation routes support both database IDs (`cm5xyz...`) and project slugs (`snipara`) as the `:projectIdOrSlug` parameter.

---
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [10793, 10805], 'relevance_score': 0.8622914425055551, 'token_count': 173, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'Route Handler Pattern', 'content': '### Route Handler Pattern

```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT: Thin route handler
export async function POST(request: Request) {
  try {
    const user = await requireAuth();
    const body = createProjectSchema.parse(await request.json());
    const result = await createProject(body, user.id);
    return successResponse(result);
  } catch (error) {
    return handleError(error);
  }
}
```

**Route handlers must:**

1. Authenticate (requireAuth)
2. Validate input (Zod schema)
3. Delegate to service/query
4. Return response

response:
Follow the project’s existing API and TypeScript standards. From the docs, the required steps and rules when adding a new API endpoint are:

Required files / steps
- Create the route file at: apps/web/src/app/api/[resource]/route.ts
- Put any DB access in lib/db/queries/ (repositories / centralized CRUD)
- Add an input validation schema using Zod
- Add tests (e.g. route.test.ts)
- Before merging run: pnpm lint && pnpm type-check && pnpm test

Route handler pattern (must follow)
- Keep the route handler thin. Pattern:
  1. Authenticate (e.g. requireAuth() — admin routes use requirePlatformAdmin())
  2. Validate input with the Zod schema
  3. Delegate business logic to a service or repository (service in lib/services/, queries in lib/db/queries/)
  4. Return a success response and handle errors (try/catch → handleError)
- Example pattern from docs:
  export async function POST(request: Request) {
    try {
      const user = await requireAuth();
      const body = schema.parse(await request.json());
      const result = await someService(body, user.id);
      return successResponse(result);
    } catch (error) {
      return handleError(error);
    }
  }

Architecture / layering rules
- Import hierarchy (lower layers cannot import from higher):
  Routes (app/api/) → Services (lib/services/) → Repositories (lib/db/queries/) → Utils & Types
- Route should delegate to services/queries; do not put business logic in the route.

TypeScript coding rules to follow
- Project uses strict TypeScript (strict: true). Ensure:
  - Explicit return types on exported functions
  - No implicit any; do not use any (use unknown + type guards or proper types)
  - Use async/await (not Promise chaining)
  - Follow import ordering: (1) external packages, (2) internal packages (@rlm-saas/*), (3) absolute imports (@/*), (4) relative imports
  - Use interface for extendable object shapes; type for unions/intersections
  - No unused locals/params

Other notes from docs
- Admin routes require requirePlatformAdmin()
- Automation routes accept either database IDs or project slugs when applicable (useful for some endpoints)

If you want, I can generate a template route.ts and a basic Zod schema and test file that follow these standards.
